title: Snow King

{{Quote|You see, our brother, the Snow King, has fallen in love with an evil frost sprite: Odra, the Duchess of Sleet. She's frozen his heart, leaving him selfish and mean.|The Leaf Queen|<ref name="tss" />}}
The '''Snow King''' was a powerful being who ruled the [[Winter/Legends|winter]] season of the [[Tree/Legends|forest]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. He could only bring about winter with the [[season scepter]]. He was the brother of the [[Leaf Queen]], the [[Sun King]], and the [[Flower Queen]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], he was seduced by [[Odra]], [[Duchess/Legends|Duchess]] of [[Water/Legends|Sleet]], and engaged in an affair with her. During this time, she froze his heart, turning him into a merciless dictator.

Taking power, he had his [[Icehead]]s confiscate the season scepter from the Sun King, and then used the scepter to cast an eternal winter over Endor. Only the efforts of the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and his allies, who were working for a coalition of the other Endor season gods, were able to stop Odra and the Snow King, and in an altercation at the [[Snow Palace]], the King's heart was thawed. Seeing the wrong he had done, the Snow King reversed his evil, and any damage he had caused was repaired.

==Biography==
===Rise to power===
[[File:SnowPalace-TSS.png|thumb|left|250px|The Snow Palace]]
Along with his siblings—the [[Leaf Queen]], [[Sun King]], and [[Flower Queen]]—the Snow King was a being of local importance on the [[Tree/Legends|forest]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], who came across a powerful [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-imbued artifact, the [[season scepter]].<ref name="coe">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}}</ref> The scepter, a powerful device, granted the user power over a particular season of the weather. Prior to or during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]], the Snow King became the ruler of the season of [[Winter/Legends|winter]]. His siblings gained power over [[Autumn/Legends|autumn]], [[Spring/Legends|spring]], and [[Summer/Legends|summer]] at the same time. A system was set up where the four siblings would rotate their seasons on a regulated basis. The Snow King took up residence in the [[Snow Palace]], a massive structure, almost impervious to attackers. From there, the Snow King would host his season. It also housed the [[Icehead]]s, beings who were loyal to him. With the use of the Sun King's season scepter, the Snow King could bring about winter at any time he deemed worthy.<ref name="tss" />

===An affair to remember===
{{Quote|Anything for you, Odra, my dear Duchess of Sleet.|The Snow King|audio=SnowKingOdra-Ewoks.flac|<ref name="tss" />}}
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="date">{{EwoksDate|appearance of the Snow King|[[The Season Scepter]]}}</ref> the Snow King met [[Duchess/Legends|Duchess]] [[Odra]] of [[Water/Legends|Sleet]]. Against the wishes of his siblings, he fell in love with her, and entered into an affair. During their tempestuous romance, the Duchess literally froze the Snow King's heart. He was not killed, however; rather, it turned him into a cold, hard dictator. His appearance also changed to reveal what Odra had done; he became a hard-edged ice-like being, as opposed to his original snow-like state. In his frozen state, the Snow King was easily manipulated and Odra used the situation to her advantage.. Practically appointing herself head of the Iceheads, Odra launched a raid on the [[Sun King's palace]].<ref name="tss" />

[[File:ColdCouple-TSS.png|thumb|right|250px|The Snow King and his mistress, the Duchess of Sleet]]
The raid was successful—the Iceheads had stolen the season scepter right from under the corpulent Sun King. Returning to the Snow Palace, Odra persuaded the King to cast an eternal blanket of snow over a large portion of Endor, most notably, the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]-inhabited [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]], with the goal of stealing the powerful [[Sunstar/Legends|Sunstar]] from them. In response, the Snow King's siblings formed a coalition, in order to form a resistance against the winter. They tried to negotiate with the Snow King, but he hardened his heart, and would not speak to them.<ref name="tss" />

===Fatal attraction===
{{Quote|You're not getting warm-hearted on me, are you? We can't have that if you're to freeze the Ewoks and take their Sunstar.|Odra|<ref name="tss" />}}
The coalition retaliated by contacting Bright Tree Village, and in particular, Ewok shaman [[Logray/Legends|Logray]]. However, their transmission went awry, and was instead received by [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]]. Warrick and his companions—Princess [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] of Bright Tree Village, shaman-in-training [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], and amateur actress [[Latara/Legends|Latara]]— took it upon themselves to travel to the Sun King's palace, and to the coalition's aid. To prevent this, Odra dispatched the Iceheads to intercept the four Ewoks. Though the raid did not turn out to be a complete success, Kneesaa, was captured, and brought to the Snow Palace. There, the Snow King froze her in a block of ice, so that she could serve as an adequate incentive for the coalition and Bright Tree to surrender to his terms.<ref name="tss" />

[[File:SnowKingMelt-TSS.png|thumb|left|250px|The Snow King falls prey to the power of the season scepter.]]
Meanwhile, back at the Sun King's palace, the coalition planned a covert operation, in which Warrick, Teebo, and Latara would infiltrate the Snow Palace, and reclaim the season scepter for the Sun King. Warrick was able to successfully gain entry to the palace by disguising himself and his friends as Iceheads; however, in the throne room, Odra was able to identify them.<ref name="tss" />

After a failed attempt to take the season scepter by enticing the Snow King with [[ice-on-a-stick]], a [[Skirmish at the Snow Palace|confrontation]] ensued, and Kneesaa was freed with a minimum of fuss. Warrick was able to wrest control of the scepter from the Snow King, and turned it against the tyrant. The scepter, in the hands of Warrick, melted the frozen heart of the King, restoring him to his former, jovial state. The scepter's power also struck Odra, who was forced to retreat. Despite losing his lover, the Snow King remained in a good mood, and asked for clemency for his past crimes, on the grounds of ignorance. The Sun King forgave his brother, and allowed him to retain his position of power.<ref name="tss" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Did I do that? I'm so sorry.|The Snow King|<ref name="tss" />}}
Before his heart was frozen, the Snow King was well liked by his siblings. When Odra froze his heart, however, he turned into a violent, cruel, and unrelenting tyrant, who would not even stop to consider the repercussions of freezing an Ewok child in ice. His political tact even altered, with him publicly moving against his siblings. When defrosted, the Snow King acted as he had before, and suffered amnesia, not even realizing the extent of his previous atrocities. He had a [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Hair/Legends|mustache]].<ref name="tss" />

==Powers and abilities==
[[File:SnowKingScepter-TSS.png|thumb|right|250px|The Snow King with the season scepter]]
The Snow King was able to utilize the powerful, Force-imbued season scepter to bring about the season of winter. He was also able to use the scepter as a ranged weapon, and also possessed the ability to bring about his brother's season of summer.<ref name="tss" />

==Equipment==
The Snow King employed the season scepter and wore blue [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]].<ref name="tss" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Snow King first appeared in "[[The Season Scepter]]," the second episode of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] television series' [[Ewoks: Season Two|second season]]. The episode was written by [[Bob Carrau]]<ref name="tss" /> and aired on [[November 1]], [[1986]].<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref>
